metadata.seed: That I'm not the one in the wrong. You know, I. I'm extremely good at my job. I mean, I am the best employee that they have ever had. Not just currently, just. I mean, just ever. I make no errors. So how can I be wrong when it comes to these circumstances with my coworkers?

It sounds like you're feeling quite confident in your abilities and that you take pride in being an exceptional employee. However, it's important to remember that everyone makes mistakes sometimes. Let's explore together if there might be other perspectives or factors at play here.

patient.query: I mean, I do want to feel confident, but it’s hard when I keep messing up. I know I should be better at this by now. Honestly, it just makes me feel like I'm not cut out for this job, like maybe I don't deserve to be here. I just wish I could shake off this feeling of being a fraud.

You may have unrealistic expectations about your performance, which could contribute to feelings of inadequacy. patient.query: I get that everyone makes mistakes, but it’s just... different for me. I feel like I should know better by now. Every time I mess up, it just reinforces that voice in my head saying I’m not good enough. I know I shouldn’t be so hard on myself, but it’s hard to shake off that nagging feeling.

I don’t want to keep feeling this way, but it’s like this cycle I can’t break out of. It just makes me want to avoid situations where I might mess up again.

patient.query: I appreciate you saying that, but it’s just hard to see it that way. I know I should probably look at it differently, but when I'm in the moment, it feels so overwhelming. I just worry about how others see me. I don’t want to let them down or, worse, for them to think I’m not capable. It just sticks with me, you know?

worry about how others see me

I don’t want to let them down
they might think I’m not capable

patient.query: I appreciate you trying to help me see it differently, but it’s just hard to shift my mindset like that. I feel like if I don’t meet those expectations, I’ll just fall short of what everyone else is doing. It’s like I have this constant pressure to perform well, and when I don’t, it just reinforces that fear of being disappointing. I wish I could just be okay with not being perfect all the time.

I have to be perfect
I'm disappointing

patient.query: Yeah, I guess it makes sense that I put a lot of pressure on myself. I just get so caught up in what I think I should be doing, and it’s hard to let go of that. Sometimes I wonder if there’s a way to just be okay with doing my best, instead of needing to be perfect. But then I get scared of what that might look like or if it’s enough.
